summary: Rough audience tape that improves mid‑set, highlighted by a scorching Scarlet>Fire and a superb China > Rider.
review: The performance is widely praised – Jerry’s guitar is on fire, the Scarlet>Fire sequence and the rare late‑70s China > Rider are called “unreal,” and the second set flows with high energy. The recording is an audience (AUD) source; the first few tracks are noisy and muffled, but sound quality clears up after the early songs and becomes quite listenable for most of the set.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is generally high (many reviewers rate 4‑5 stars for performance), but recording quality is uneven with many low‑star complaints about the early audio, pulling the overall average down.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Very noisy and muffled start (first ~1–2 tracks), PA hiss and cassette‑generation artifacts; sound clears up around “Candyman”/mid‑set and remains decent through the second half.

standout_songs: Estimated Prophet > Eyes of the World
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is present but suffers from poor audio; second set is praised for its energy, seamless transitions (e.g., Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World) and standout jams.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly lauded for a “smoking” lead‑guitar performance throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Keys: Mentioned only in passing (Keith’s final days) – no specific playing comments.
